summary: Sprint 2 (AI Bulk Inventory Import) backend tests: 15/15 passed. CSV/PNG/XLSX AI extraction via Claude Sonnet 4.5 all return normalised UPPERCASE brand-first product names with confidence and field_confidence. Error paths (empty=400, >15MB=413, unauth=401) work. Bulk-inward inserts rows, auto-creates products, skips invalid rows (empty product or qty<=0), and rejects empty payloads with 400. Multi-tenant isolation verified between two companies. Existing Phase 1/2 smoke endpoints still green.

minor.issue: Inserts items as inward_entries but does NOT update the file_id/storage for the source upload; ai-extract result is not persisted on the server (only logged via activity_logs). If the user wants to re-preview a batch later, they must re-upload. Consider persisting extract_results collection keyed by batch_label.

Notification call uses audience='Admin' (capitalised) while the rest of the codebase consistently uses lowercase 'admin' (e.g. register/login flows). Admin users currently filter notifications by company_id only so this still works, but it is inconsistent — non-admins may eventually see this notification if filter logic ever changes.
Sets `model` in response as a hard-coded string 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' even if extractor fails partway / returns empty. Acceptable but could mislead clients.
Reads the entire upload into memory before size-check; a malicious 1GB upload would still be buffered. Consider streaming/early Content-Length check.

action_items: (LOW) Make notification audience casing consistent: change `push_notification(cid, 'Admin', ...)` in /api/inventory/bulk-inward (server.py ~line 1019) to lowercase 'admin' to match the rest of the codebase.

(LOW, separate from this sprint) Pre-existing bug from iteration_1 not yet fixed: employee_id login still lowercases the identifier and never matches the uppercase-stored employee_id (server.py /api/auth/login).
(OPTIONAL) Persist extraction results so a user can revisit a preview without re-uploading and re-spending an LLM call.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1050+ lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Split into routers (auth, clients, inventory, ai_extract, tasks, employees, notifications).
ai_inventory_extractor._call_claude_vision/_text duplicate ~12 lines (chat init + stream loop). Extract a helper `_stream_to_text(chat, user_msg)`.

server.py /api/inventory/ai-extract: error message echoes raw exception text to the client (`f"Extraction failed: {str(e)[:200]}"`). Could leak internal details (model name, paths). Consider returning a generic 500 with a server-side log only.
server.py BulkInwardIn has no max-rows guard; a malicious client could submit 1M rows and trigger a huge insert_many. Add `max_items=2000` or similar Pydantic validator.
server.py /api/inventory/bulk-inward uses `await ensure_product(...)` inside a for-loop — N round-trips to Mongo. For large batches consider de-duplicating product names and bulk-upserting.
ai_inventory_extractor.MODEL_NAME hard-coded; consider env var so the model can be flipped without redeploy.
extract_from_excel reads max 500 rows but silently drops the rest — should surface in `notes` field and ideally in `raw_row_count` warning.
seed_data_creation: Tests created: ~3 inward_entries with import_batch starting with TEST_BATCH_*, plus auto-created products WAAREE PANEL 540W, ADANI INVERTER 5KW, VALID CABLE on the primary tenant; a second tenant 'Isolation EPC' is registered with a unique email per run (isolation-{uuid}@solarix.com) — these accumulate in MongoDB across test runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 2 AI bulk import endpoints (ai-extract, bulk-inward) are fully verified for CSV/PNG/XLSX, error cases, isolation, and integration with products+inward collections. AI calls take ~15-25s each; allow 180s timeout per call. Each run creates one fresh secondary tenant — periodic DB cleanup of `isolation-*@solarix.com` users is advised. Pre-existing employee_id-login bug (iteration_1) is still unresolved and out of scope for this sprint.
rca of the issue: No new issues found; existing iteration_1 employee_id login bug RCA still valid (login() lower-cases identifier but employee_id is stored uppercase).
